directive.title: RBAC: role field on users and RFQ route authorization

auditor.summary: The build implements the core RBAC authorization flow correctly with proper audit logging on denial, direct DB writes (no HTTP fetch), and appropriate route protection. However, it has several critical issues: (1) The migration uses PostgreSQL ENUM type instead of the specified VARCHAR(20) with CHECK constraint, which is a spec violation. (2) The authOptions are redefined inline with empty providers instead of importing from the existing auth configuration, which will cause runtime failures. (3) TypeScript strict mode is violated with 'any' types. (4) Tests use vitest which is not in the project dependencies. (5) The audit_logs immutable column handling is incomplete. The build is recoverable with fixes but cannot pass in its current state.

top_issues.description: Migration uses PostgreSQL ENUM type instead of VARCHAR(20) with CHECK constraint as specified in the build spec. The spec explicitly requires 'VARCHAR(20), NOT NULL, DEFAULT ''viewer'', CHECK (role IN (''admin'', ''manager'', ''viewer''))'. Using a custom enum type is a different approach that may cause compatibility issues with the Drizzle schema definition.

Uses pgEnum('user_role_enum', ...) but the migration creates the enum type. If migration is changed to use VARCHAR(20) as specified, this file becomes incompatible. The spec says to place enum declaration here but also says migration should use VARCHAR(20) with CHECK constraint - contradictory. The Drizzle schema should use text() with a check constraint or a custom type that matches the migration.

Uses 'any' types in authOptions callbacks (lines 17-18, 22-23) which violates TypeScript strict mode requirement. The spec explicitly states 'TypeScript strict mode must be satisfied — no ''any'' types'.
